_comment: RSS/Atom feeds per interest id. Fully editable. NB: some niche sites (e.g. actuaries.digital, theactuary.com) block scripted requests behind a WAF — use feeds that serve scripts (Google News RSS queries, government/regulator feeds like APRA work well).
_comment_validating: A LOCAL `node scripts/ingest.mjs --dry-run` DOES NOT validate these feeds. The daily run fetches from a GitHub Actions runner (Azure IP), and some publishers answer that IP differently: on 2026-07-17 a local run reported 45 fetched / 0 FAILED while the same commit in CI silently dropped four feeds with 403. Feeds are only truly validated from CI — see `gh run view <id> --log | grep ✗` on a generate.yml run. Verified from a runner that day: Substack (any publication) 403s EVERY User-Agent, including no UA and its /api/v1/archive JSON — a hard IP ban on GitHub's range, not fixable with headers. nofilmschool.com does the opposite: it 403s a Chrome UA (a browser cannot exist in a datacenter, so it scores as a liar) and serves an honest bot UA, which is why ingest.mjs retries a 403 once as CortexReader.
_comment_blocked: Substack 403s every UA from GitHub runner IPs (measured 2026-07-17: browser, honest bot, curl, none — and the /api/v1/archive JSON API too). Since 2026-07-18 the actuarial Substacks (invisiblebalancesheet, actuarialnotes) live in data/sources-local.json instead: scripts/ingest-local.mjs fetches them from the reader PC on a schedule and pushes full-body pool items. NEVER move them back here — they would fail every cloud ingest and page the health check. finance/boredombaron.substack.com is blocked the same way but IS well indexed (27 items), so it lives on below as a Google News mirror.
_comment_youtube: YouTube channels are valid sources: add https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=CHANNEL_ID — items are tagged kind:"video" with the channel as source. Find a channel_id by opening the channel and reading the UC… id from page source, or fetch the feed URL to confirm it is not a 404. The feed carries title + description only; the scheduled LOCAL run (scripts/ingest-local.mjs) enriches pending videos with their real transcript overnight (transcript_at stamp) — YouTube bot-walls GitHub runner IPs so this cannot happen cloud-side. Name any channel you add in a comment: a bare UC id is unmaintainable and dead ids have failed silently for weeks.

with the channel as source. Find a channel_id by opening the channel and reading the UC… id from page source, or fetch the feed URL to confirm it isn't a 404. The feed gives title + description only (no transcript), so videos seed the pool as leads to weave in with attribution, not full-text sources.
_comment_software_ai: 8 feeds is a CEILING, not a target — do not add a 9th without removing one. DIGEST_PER_INTEREST=8 and capRoundRobin's round 0 breaks the moment it fills the budget, so with 9+ ACTIVE feeds the later rounds never run and the least-frequent feeds silently get ZERO slots. Queue order is newest-item-first, so the feeds evicted are exactly the low-volume mechanism ones below — the same way a trade wire once buried the Actuaries Institute. Expected simultaneously-active here is ~6.4/8, which leaves headroom. REMOVED 2026-07-17 and don't restore without a plan: theverge.com (consumer tech: 'Samsung's 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper') and a broad Google-News 'artificial intelligence OR software engineering OR machine learning' query (33/month, e.g. 'Xi Jinping: AI Must Be Symphony of International Cooperation') — measured, those two WERE the 5-of-8 news drift, and they out-publish every mechanism feed so no ladder survives while they're here. The reader's 'keep current with AI/dev' half is now served in a CRAFT register (Addy Osmani, Fireship, Raroque) instead of a consumer-news one. YouTube channel_ids: UCsBjURrPoezykLs9EqgamOA = Fireship, UCXl4i9dYBrFOabk0xGmbkRA = Dwarkesh Patel (NB near-dormant — 1 video/90d), UC4x3CR25WSlvMJUtSPPzwwg = Chris Raroque (solo dev shipping productivity apps; AI coding workflow — a reader pick). Name any channel you add: a bare UC id is unmaintainable and dead ids have failed silently for weeks. Raroque fits indie-income too, but do NOT list this feed under two interests: seen.json dedupes by guid GLOBALLY, so the first interest in config order claims every item and the second gets none — let the author cross-tag via #meta.interests instead. ALSO REMOVED 2026-07-17: hnrss.org/frontpage — it was taking 2 of 8 digest slots with 0 of 3 pooled items about his stack ('Trump Media to sell instant access', 'EEG shows brain can encode two speech streams', 'Pebble Mega Update'), and the profile asks for deepen-first, NOT random exploration. Aiming it was tried and failed: hnrss's ?q= filter does not bind (a Next.js/Postgres/React/Supabase query still returns 'Canada says bridge tolls' and 'M 3.9 Explosion, Florida'), and ?points=150 only buys popular, not relevant. Serendipity now comes from Fireship/Dwarkesh/Addy, which are adjacent rather than random. Adjacent candidates that were fetch-tested and REJECTED, don't retry blind: react.dev (200 but 143d stale, ~2 posts/yr), vercel.com/atom (1352-item changelog, ~2/day), supabase.com/rss.xml + ionic.io/blog/rss (his stack, but release marketing), tkdodo.eu + isquaredsoftware (volume claims false on measurement), sentry.io (8.6/mo firehose), planetscale (6.1MB/7.8s fetch), kentcdodds/robinwieruch/antfu/totaltypescript/highscalability (all stale or dead).
_comment_actuarial: The Actuaries Institute is the anchor source. It publishes no RSS (actuaries.digital folded into actuaries.asn.au; /feed and every sitemap path 503), so the first two entries are its public keyless Kontent.ai Delivery API — richer than RSS: editor-written description, real dates, and a practice_areas taxonomy (Life Insurance, Superannuation and Investments, …) carried onto the pool item as `topics`. Entry 1 = system.type=resource, the POLICY LIBRARY (submissions, dialogue/discussion papers, reports, media releases) — the profession's own positions, ~8/month, PDFs on content.actuaries.asn.au which ingest.mjs extracts text from. Entry 2 = system.type=article, the Actuaries Digital magazine. Entry 3 catches press coverage of Institute research that neither API carries.
_comment_actuarial_taxonomy: GOTCHA: content_types is a NESTED taxonomy and Kontent's [any] filter does NOT roll up child terms, so the policy query must list CHILD codenames explicitly — filtering on the 'Publication' parent silently returns zero Submissions. The terms below are: Submission, Dialogue Paper, Discussion Paper, Public Policy Position Statement, Report (all children of Publication), plus top-level Papers and Media Release. Deliberately excluded: Job Ad, Presentation slides, Podcast, Photos, Video, and the Education/Governance/Membership document trees. Re-derive with: curl https://deliver.kontent.ai/4f4649d8-6df9-023d-bb6c-14e842b8aadb/taxonomies/content_types
